content: Most existing explorations of Self-Refine and LLM self-reflection mechanisms treat self-feedback as a unidirectional correction loop, but we can reframe their combination as a multi-perspective self-interrogation system: Self-Refine's iterative refinement pipeline can be anchored to the inconsistent divergent perspectives leveraged by modern LLM self-reflection mechanisms, to resolve the blind spot of single-perspective self-feedback that often overlooks logical flaws. This creates a self-improving system that retains Self-Refine's core advantage of requiring no extra training or supervision while fixing its common failure mode of converging to a locally optimal wrong output.
